key_points.text: Compute Networking segment revenue grew 88% year-over-year to $74,550 million in Q1 FY2027 (ended April 26, 2026) from $39,589 million in Q1 FY2026 (ended April 27, 2025)

citation.quote: Compute Networking segment includes our Data Center accelerated computing and networking platforms and AI solutions and software, and automotive platforms and autonomous and electric vehicle solutions including software. Three Months Ended Apr 26, 2026 Revenue $ 74,550... Three Months Ended Apr 27, 2025 Revenue $ 39,589

key_points.text: Operating income for Compute Networking surged 142% to $53,335 million in Q1 FY2027 from $22,054 million in Q1 FY2026, demonstrating strong operational leverage
Total company revenue reached $81,615 million in Q1 FY2027 compared to $44,062 million in Q1 FY2026, representing 85% year-over-year growth
NVIDIA introduced NVIDIA NVLink Fusion in fiscal 2026 to enable hyperscalers and custom ASIC designers to integrate custom CPUs and XPUs with the platform, expanding addressable market

citation.quote: In fiscal year 2026, we introduced NVIDIA NVLink Fusion to enable hyperscalers and custom ASIC designers to integrate custom CPUs and XPUs with our platform.

key_points.text: Company launched and scaled the Blackwell Ultra platform in fiscal 2026, optimized for agentic, reasoning, and physical AI, delivering significant increases in token throughput and cost per token reductions versus Hopper generation

citation.quote: In fiscal year 2026, we launched and scaled the NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra platform, optimized for agentic, reasoning, and physical AI. Building on the architectural breakthroughs of Blackwell and leveraging Dynamo inference software, it delivers a significant increase in token throughput and reduction in cost per token compared to the Hopper generation.

key_points.text: Unveiled NVIDIA Rubin platform for fiscal 2027 with production shipments expected in H2 FY2027, designed for agentic AI with up to 10x reduction in cost per token versus Blackwell

citation.quote: In fiscal year 2026, we unveiled the NVIDIA Rubin platform, which is expected to commence production shipments in the second half of fiscal year 2027. Built for agentic AI and reasoning, it excels at processing multi-step problem-solving and massive long-context workflows, delivering up to a 10x reduction in cost per token compared to Blackwell.

key_points.text: Over 7.5 million developers worldwide using CUDA and other software tools, demonstrating broad ecosystem expansion and platform stickiness

citation.quote: There are over 7.5 million developers worldwide using CUDA and our other software tools to

key_points.text: NVIDIA powers over 78% of supercomputers on the global TOP500 list, including 9 of the top 10 systems on the Green500 list, establishing dominant market position

citation.quote: Including GPUs and networking, NVIDIA powers over 78% of the supercomputers on the global TOP500 list, including 9 of the top 10 systems on the Green500 list.

key_points.text: Increased quarterly cash dividend from $0.01 per share to $0.25 per share on May 18, 2026, reflecting confidence in cash generation and shareholder returns

citation.quote: On May 18, 2026, we increased our quarterly cash dividend from $ 0.01 per share to $ 0.25 per share to all shareholders of record on June 4, 2026.
